quiz1.missionText: Refresh the news to get the latest updates

insight.title: How did you know pulling down would refresh the news?
insight.subtitle: Pulling down is the universal refresh gesture
insight.pullDesc: Pulling down on a list screen is a refresh gesture popularized by Twitter and mail apps. It is now adopted by apps worldwide.
insight.spinnerDesc: The rotating circular indicator is a common visual language used worldwide in apps to indicate processing or loading.
insight.feedTitle: Feeds always need the latest info
insight.feedDesc: Information feed screens like news, social media, and mail are designed to trigger a manual refresh when pulled from top to bottom.

quiz2.missionText: Check the top news in the Sports category

insight.title: How did you know swiping sideways would switch categories?
insight.subtitle: Tabs are a sign of horizontally arranged pages
insight.tabDesc: Tabs lined up at the top are a table of contents for categories. It is standard design to switch between them by swiping horizontally like turning pages.
insight.swipeTitle: Moving a finger sideways feels like turning a page
insight.swipeDesc: Swiping left and right like turning the pages of a book is the most natural page-switching gesture in mobile apps.
insight.labelTitle: Tab labels point to the target category
insight.labelDesc: You can reach the information you need by tapping the tab labeled Sports or swiping to it.

quiz3.missionText: The text in this article is too small to read. Change the font size to Large.

insight.title: How did you know the Aa icon could change the font size?
insight.subtitle: Aa is the universal icon for font settings
insight.aaTitle: Aa is the world-standard icon for changing font size
insight.aaDesc: An icon showing an uppercase A and lowercase a together is adopted by browsers and apps worldwide as a symbol for font size and display settings.
insight.accessDesc: Many apps have a feature that lets you adjust text visibility yourself when the text is too small to read comfortably.
insight.sizeTitle: Small, medium, large is the classic set
insight.sizeDesc: Font size options with three levels of small, medium, and large are the standard. It is standard design to let users choose according to their preference or eyesight.

quiz4.missionText: Hide the movie review article that contains spoilers from the list

insight.title: How did you know long pressing would let you hide the article?
insight.subtitle: Long press is a gesture that reveals hidden options
insight.longPressTitle: Long press opens additional menu options
insight.longPressDesc: Pressing and holding an icon or item to reveal additional options is an interaction pattern common to both Android and iOS.
insight.menuTitle: The ... icon is a sign of hidden actions
insight.menuDesc: The ellipsis or vertical three-dot icon is a universal UI symbol meaning More Options. Tapping it expands a menu.
insight.hideTitle: Control your information with the hide feature
insight.hideDesc: The ability to individually hide articles and notifications you do not want to see is a standard feature of modern news apps and social media.
